# Accessibility Requirements Quality Checklist: Website Improvements

**Purpose**: Validate that accessibility requirements (forms, controls, focus, assistive tech) are complete, unambiguous, and testable. Tests the quality of what is specified, not implementation behavior.  
**Created**: 2026-02-28  
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Is the newsletter email field required to have both an associated label (or accessible name) and success/error messages in a live region, or is one of these left implicit? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK002 Are all required fields of the contact form explicitly listed so that label and announcement requirements can be verified per field? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK003 Is the header tab order (logo, nav, social, theme toggle) explicitly required as the single canonical order, or could implementations vary? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK004 Are requirements specified for how the theme toggle exposes current state (e.g. aria-pressed, aria-label including mode, or other) so assistive tech can announce it? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008]
- [ ] CHK005 Are accessibility requirements defined for the project filter/group UI (keyboard operability, focus, labels) when that feature is implemented? [Gap, Spec §User Story 3]
- [ ] CHK006 Are accessibility requirements specified for the on-site search input and results (focus management, announcements, keyboard use)? [Gap, Spec §FR-012]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is "associated label or accessible name" for the newsletter field defined (e.g. visible &lt;label&gt;, aria-label, or aria-labelledby) so implementers know the minimum? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "announced via a live region" specified with region type or politeness (e.g. aria-live="polite", role="status") so messages are reliably announced? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "visible focus indicator" defined with at least one measurable criterion (e.g. contrast ratio, ring width, or reference to a standard)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK010 Is "expose current state (e.g. dark or light)" for the theme toggle required to be programmatically determinable (e.g. via accessibility tree), not only visually? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK011 Are label and live region requirements for the newsletter form consistent with those for the contact form (same level of specificity)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, FR-006]
- [ ] CHK012 Are focus and keyboard requirements for the header consistent with any other global interactive elements (e.g. skip link, footer links)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK013 Can SC-002 ("screen reader user can complete newsletter signup and contact form and understand all success and error messages") be verified without implementation details (e.g. with defined assistive tech and steps)? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK014 Can SC-003 ("keyboard-only user can reach and operate all header controls with visible focus and correct tab order") be objectively tested? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK015 Is the acceptance scenario "theme control exposes current state" testable (e.g. by inspecting accessibility tree or using a screen reader)? [Measurability, Spec §User Story 2 Acceptance 4]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK016 Are requirements specified for keyboard-only navigation through the newsletter and contact flows (tab order, submit activation, error recovery)? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2]
- [ ] CHK017 Are requirements defined for screen reader users when validation fails (inline error association, focus management, or announcement)? [Coverage, Spec §FR-005, FR-006]
- [ ] CHK018 Is reduced-motion preference for motion (FR-014) explicitly tied to accessibility success criteria or only to performance/stability? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK019 When the theme toggle has no stored preference and uses system preference, is the initial state required to be exposed to assistive tech without a flash? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK020 If the contact or newsletter form has a honeypot or other hidden field, are requirements specified so it is hidden from assistive tech (e.g. aria-hidden) and does not break focus order? [Edge Case, Gap]

## Automation & Validation

- [ ] CHK021 Are automated accessibility checks (FR-019) required to cover the same interactive elements as the manual acceptance scenarios (newsletter, contact, header, theme toggle)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-019, User Story 2]
- [ ] CHK022 Is a standard or level (e.g. WCAG 2.1 AA) referenced for accessibility requirements so automation and manual testing can align? [Gap]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- These items validate the quality of accessibility requirements as written; they do not verify that the site is accessible.
- Each `/speckit.checklist` run creates a new file; this file focuses on accessibility only.
